codeReview.summary: The EE feature adds a bounded curve-curve intersection routine with line-segment coverage, but it does not yet satisfy the feature contract for multiple intersections or persistence of EE interference parameters in `BopDs`.

issues.description: `intersect_ee` returns immediately after the first Newton-refined hit, so it can never report additional valid intersections for the same edge pair. That violates the feature's expected behavior for multiple intersections such as circle-circle or other multi-hit curves.

The test suite only covers line-segment cases and parameter bounds; it does not include any multi-intersection curved case despite the feature requirement calling out cases like circle-circle. As written, the implementation's sampled fallback is unproven for the core non-linear behavior the feature claims to support.

`EEInterference` stores only the two edge IDs, with no fields for the intersection parameters on either curve. The feature contract explicitly requires storing parameters for both curves, so the data model cannot represent the required result.

`BopDs` exports no EE interference API and has no `push_ee_interference`/`ee_interferences` accessors, so the feature still does not persist EE interferences into the shared boolean-operation data structure. This matches the worker handoff note that EE storage was left unwired.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `rust-bop-worker` skill still instructs workers to run `cargo clippy -p truck-bop` and address warnings, but mission guidance and `.factory/services.yaml` already document that clippy is intentionally skipped because of pre-existing workspace issues outside `truck-bop`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Volumes/DPC/work/cad/truck-new/.factory/skills/rust-bop-worker/SKILL.md` step 4 says to run clippy and address warnings; `/Users/dongpengcheng/.factory/missions/28558208-113c-4faf-9654-e1aaded1d9ab/AGENTS.md` and `/Volumes/DPC/work/cad/truck-new/.factory/services.yaml` both note clippy is known-broken / skipped.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture note says interference tables track `EE` records, but it does not spell out that EE records must include both edge parameters. That omission likely contributed to adding an `EEInterference` type that cannot store the required parametric data.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Volumes/DPC/work/cad/truck-new/.factory/library/architecture.md` lists EE interference tables generically, while `/Volumes/DPC/work/cad/truck-new/truck-bop/src/bopds/interference.rs:38` defines `EEInterference` with only `edge1` and `edge2`.

summary: Reviewed commit `58011a4f1c6262aeae8bc316ccc2572b43af73d9`, the worker handoff, transcript skeleton, and current EE-related source. The implementation covers basic line-segment hits and no-hit cases, but it still fails the feature contract because it returns at most one intersection, lacks curved multi-hit test coverage, and does not persist EE intersection parameters in `BopDs`.
